title: The July Cash Gap That Catches NDIS Providers Every Year
linkedin:
Every July, NDIS providers absorb a wage increase the moment it lands. The pricing update that should offset it arrives weeks later, at different speeds for each participant type.

The SCHADS Award rolls over from the first full pay period of July, so the labour cost is immediate. The NDIS Pricing Arrangements update flows through at three different paces: agency-managed participants can be updated from July 1, plan-managed participants involve a processing lag of one to several weeks while plan managers work through the same transition period, and self-managed participants update at their own pace.

Most providers carry a mix of all three, which means July and early August margins are structurally lower every year. Not because something went wrong, but because of how the timing lines up.

The providers who come through July well are the ones who know their participant mix before June 30. That data makes two questions answerable: which support categories are generating positive margin at current price limits, and which participant plans are due for renewal before their funding exhausts.

Gaps in participant plans create gaps in revenue. Knowing which plans are approaching their end date, and having conversations with support coordinators before June 30, is one of the most direct working capital improvements available going into the new financial year.

The July pressure returns every year. The EOFY accounts, read properly, contain the information needed to plan for it. The difference is whether that reading happens in June or in August, after the margin gap has already opened.

Full piece on the ProfitPulse blog.

NDIS providers: July is two weeks away, and the timing gap between the SCHADS Award increase and the NDIS pricing update is about to arrive on schedule.

The wage cost lands on the first full pay period. The pricing flows through at different speeds depending on each participant's management type. For many providers, that means a gap of several weeks across parts of the portfolio before new rates can actually be claimed.

Knowing your participant mix before June 30 changes how the July result looks.

We've written through the mechanics and what to look at in the EOFY accounts: https://profit-pulse.com.au/the-july-cash-gap-that-catches-ndis-providers-every-year

google:
New blog post: The July Cash Gap That Catches NDIS Providers Every Year

July 1 brings two events at the same time for NDIS providers across Queensland, NSW, and Victoria: an immediate wage increase under the SCHADS Award, and a pricing update that flows through to accounts at different speeds depending on how each participant's plan is managed.

Agency-managed participants can be updated from July 1. Plan-managed participants involve a processing lag of one to several weeks. Self-managed participants update at their own pace. Most providers carry a mix of all three, which means July and early August margins are structurally lower until all plans are across the new pricing.

The EOFY is the natural moment to review this clearly. Which support categories are generating positive margin at current price limits? Which participant plans are due for renewal before their funding exhausts? What does the participant management mix mean for cash timing in July and August?

These are questions the June 30 accounts can answer, with the full year's data available.

For NDIS and disability services providers across Queensland and NSW, the next two weeks are the window to work through this before the July pressure arrives.

Read the full breakdown on the ProfitPulse blog: https://profit-pulse.com.au/the-july-cash-gap-that-catches-ndis-providers-every-year
